ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for white bread with chocolate spread and peanut butter; mapped to closest parent bread food product class (FOODON_03315091).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for white bread with chocolate spread and peanut butter; mapped to the closest parent 'Bread and similar products' category (A000E).
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for this composite bread dish; left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.description: 2 slices of white bread with chocolate spread and peanut butter
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving consists of two slices of white bread (approximately 100g total) spread with chocolate spread and peanut butter, commonly eaten as a breakfast or snack.

nutrient_source.title: White bread with chocolate and peanut butter — HPB Singapore Foodo Database (F05020124)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo record F05020124 for white bread with chocolate and peanut butter. All core per_100g values are sourced from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy (white bread + chocolate hazelnut spread + peanut butter) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; white bread GI reference value
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for white bread with chocolate spread and peanut butter as a composite dish. GI of 65 is assigned based on the University of Sydney GI Database reference value for white bread (GI ~70), moderated downward to approximately 65 to account for the fat and protein contribution of peanut butter, which is well-established in the literature to blunt postprandial glucose response (Brand-Miller et al., 2008; Diabetes Care). The input record's embedded GI of 40 (confidence 0.307) was reviewed and rejected as insufficiently supported — a GI of 40 would classify this as low-GI, which is inconsistent with the refined white bread base dominating the carbohydrate fraction. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 45.28) / 100 = 29.4 per 100g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 380mg/100g combined with high sugar (10.63g/100g) and low potassium-to-sodium ratio (potassium 156mg vs sodium 380mg)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this food due to its high sodium content (380mg/100g), unfavourable potassium-to-sodium ratio, and added sugar from chocolate spread, all of which independently and synergistically elevate blood pressure risk; substituting with wholegrain bread, natural unsalted peanut butter, and omitting chocolate spread would substantially improve the cardiovascular profile.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load (45.28g/100g) from white bread base with added sugar (10.63g/100g) from chocolate spread, yielding a high glycemic load of 29.4 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this food because the refined white bread base delivers a high glycemic load (GL 29.4/100g serving), and the chocolate spread adds 10.63g of sugar per 100g; while peanut butter provides protein and fat that partially moderate glucose response, the overall carbohydrate and sugar burden is likely to cause significant postprandial hyperglycaemia, and replacement with wholegrain bread and natural peanut butter without chocolate spread is strongly recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~3.2g/100g estimated) from chocolate spread and peanut butter, combined with high sodium (380mg/100g) and added sugar (10.63g/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease, this food presents a moderate-to-high concern due to its combination of elevated sodium, added sugar from chocolate spread, and estimated saturated fat from the spread components; peanut butter contributes beneficial monounsaturated fats and protein, but the overall profile — particularly sodium and sugar — warrants limiting portion size and frequency, and choosing low-sodium, no-added-sugar alternatives where possible.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar (10.63g/100g) from chocolate spread promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; refined carbohydrate base contributes to glycaemic inflammatory load
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit this food as the chocolate spread adds sugar that promotes inflammatory cytokine production and the refined white bread base contributes to glycaemic inflammatory load; natural peanut butter on wholegrain bread without chocolate spread would be a significantly less pro-inflammatory alternative.

llm_training.natural_language_description: White bread with chocolate spread and peanut butter is a popular assembled bread-based snack or breakfast item consisting of sliced white bread topped or filled with a chocolate hazelnut-style spread and peanut butter. The white bread provides a soft, mild-flavoured refined carbohydrate base, while the chocolate spread contributes sweetness, cocoa flavour, and added sugar, and the peanut butter adds a rich, nutty, slightly salty flavour along with protein, fat, and dietary fibre. The combination is widely consumed in Singapore and internationally as a quick breakfast, school snack, or afternoon snack. Nutritionally, per 100g the food provides 344 kcal, 11.69g protein, 13.4g fat, 45.28g carbohydrate, 10.63g sugar, 3.19g dietary fibre, and 380mg sodium. The high refined carbohydrate content and added sugar from the chocolate spread make this food unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes or hypertension. The peanut butter component provides beneficial protein and monounsaturated fats, partially moderating the glycaemic response, but the overall glycemic load remains high at approximately 29.4 per 100g serving. The food contains gluten (from white bread) and peanuts, making it unsuitable for individuals with coeliac disease or peanut allergy. It is vegetarian but not vegan due to potential milk derivatives in the chocolate spread. Calcium content is notably high at 178mg/100g, likely from fortified white bread, and iron is 4.4mg/100g. This food is best consumed occasionally and in small portions, with wholegrain bread and natural unsalted peanut butter without chocolate spread recommended as a healthier alternative.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo record F05020124 with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy and documented. GI revised from the unsupported embedded value (0.307 confidence) to a defensible analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database white bread reference, with fat/protein moderation documented. All other fields are complete and internally consistent.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient vitamins, and moisture data for this composite bread dish are not individually reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database record F05020124, and no sufficiently close composite entry was found in USDA FoodData Central to provide these values with confidence. Saturated fat (3.2g/100g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy (white bread + chocolate hazelnut spread + peanut butter weighted proportionally) and should be verified against a direct laboratory analysis. The input record's GI value of 40 (confidence 0.307, source 'Embedded GI Database') was rejected as insufficiently supported and inconsistent with the refined white bread base; GI revised to 65 based on University of Sydney GI Database white bread reference with documented fat/protein moderation rationale. The dietary tag 'high_fibre' in the input record is questionable given 3.19g dietary fibre/100g, which is below the Singapore HPB high-fibre threshold of 4g/100g for solid foods; this tag has been removed from the output. Cholesterol value of 0.29mg/100g is retained from HPB source as reported. Local-language names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) have been added as this food is widely consumed in Singapore; these are descriptive translations of the English name rather than culturally specific dish names.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is precise canonical English name 'White Bread with Chocolate Spread and Peanut Butter'. Local-language names now present for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi, satisfying G6 multilingual compliance for a food widely consumed in Singapore.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (344.0), protein_g (11.69), fat_g (13.4), carbohydrate_g (45.28) all present, numeric, and sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo F05020124 with confidence 0.95. nutrient_source fully documented.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (3.19), sodium_mg (380.0), saturated_fat_g (3.2 estimated and documented), sugar_g (10.63) all present as numeric values. Saturated fat estimation basis documented in notes and nutrient_source.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (65), gi_category ('medium'), glycemic_load_per_serving (29.4), gi_source all present. GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate (white bread reference) with fat/protein moderation rationale documented. Input embedded GI of 40 (confidence 0.307) rejected and replacement rationale documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn closest_parent match to FOODON_03315091 (bread food product) is defensible and honest. FoodEx2 closest_parent match to A000E (Bread and similar products) is defensible. Both match_type values now correctly set to 'closest_parent' per allowed values. SNOMED CT left null with gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well above the 200-character minimum. Covers food identity, preparation, taste/texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, key nutrients, allergens, and dietary flags.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (high concern), Type 2 Diabetes (high concern, human_condition_name correctly set to 'Type 2 Diabetes'), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate concern). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. Rheumatoid arthritis included as additional condition.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases array contains 12 entries covering canonical name, alternate English forms, common brand-reference variant (Nutella), abbreviated forms, and local-language aliases in Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi. All strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in the flat aliases array. Self-check performed: every value in aliases_by_language verified present in flat aliases array.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 3: Added mandatory local-language names to local_names for Chinese simplified (白面包配巧克力酱和花生酱), Chinese traditional (白麵包配巧克力醬和花生醬), Malay (Roti putih dengan coklat spread dan mentega kacang), Tamil (வெள்ளை ரொட்டி சாக்லேட் ஸ்பிரெட் மற்றும் வேர்க்கடலை வெண்ணெய்), and Hindi (चॉकलेट स्प्रेड और मूंगफली के मक्खन के साथ सफेद ब्रेड) to satisfy G6 and Q1 multilingual compliance.
Iteration 3: Added all local-language names to aliases_by_language (chinese, malay, tamil, hindi) and to the flat aliases array to satisfy Q8 aliases consistency rule.
Iteration 3: Changed foodon_match_type and foodex2_match_type from 'broader' to 'closest_parent' to satisfy G5 ontology honesty gate (allowed values: 'closest_parent', 'exact', 'none').
Iteration 3: All -1 values in per_100g are explicitly documented in the 'notes' field with reason for data unavailability and sources consulted, satisfying G3 numeric hygiene.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record passes all 6 hard gates and scores 8/8 on the Q1-Q8 scorecard. All previous REJECT reasons have been resolved: G3 numeric hygiene satisfied with full -1 documentation in notes; G5 ontology honesty satisfied with match_type values corrected to 'closest_parent'; G6 multilingual naming compliance satisfied with Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names added; Q1 food identity satisfied; Q8 alias coverage satisfied with all local-language aliases present in both aliases_by_language and flat aliases array. Core nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo F05020124 (confidence 0.95). GI revised from unsupported embedded value to defensible analogous estimate of 65 from University of Sydney GI Database. Health context covers all three required conditions with specific clinical guidance.
